rules.intro: A prediction game built as an homage to the World Cup's own format — groups, knockouts, and a final.
rules.step1: Pick the winner of each match
rules.step2: Guess the exact score for bonus points
rules.format_desc_1: The Format classification is an homage to the World Cup's own structure. You're placed in a group. After each stage is finalised, the bottom players are eliminated and the survivors are redrawn into new groups. Points reset to zero at each stage — only your current performance matters.
rules.format_desc_2: With 48 players, the elimination curve mirrors the World Cup itself. With fewer players, the curve is adjusted to keep every round competitive.
rules.group_stage_desc_1: All three matchdays count as one stage. Your points accumulate across MD1, MD2, and MD3.
rules.group_stage_desc_2: After MD3, the cut works like the real World Cup:
rules.third_place: **3rd place** is the danger zone. Your points are compared against every other 3rd-place finisher across all groups. Only the best thirds survive — the rest are eliminated. With 48 players: 8 of 12 thirds qualify. The other 4 go home.
rules.knockout_desc: Points reset. New groups drawn from survivors. Bottom half eliminated. Repeat until only the finalists remain.
rules.r32_meta: 32 survive · points reset · new groups drawn
rules.knockout_note: Points reset to zero. Only this round counts for survival.
rules.final_desc: The last 2 survivors predict both the Third-Place Play-Off and the Final. Most points wins. No elimination — just a winner.
rules.narrowing_desc: With 48 players, the elimination curve mirrors the World Cup itself. With fewer players, adjustments are made for competitive integrity — ensuring at least one elimination at every stage.
locks.title: When do picks lock?
locks.desc_1: Each match locks 10 minutes before kickoff. You can submit and change your picks right up until then — once it locks, it's final.
locks.desc_2: Changed your mind? No problem — update your picks as many times as you like before that match locks.
locks.detail_1: Outcome-only predictions are saved, but you still need to add exact scores to fully complete that day's predictions.
locks.detail_2: Events inside the same tournament stage may lock on different days and at different times.
faq.title: FAQs
faq.q_every_match: Do I have to predict every match?
faq.a_every_match: No. You can skip any match. But every correct pick earns points, so the more you predict the better your chances on the leaderboard.
faq.a_exact_required: No — it's optional. Pick the winner (or draw) first. If you also nail the exact score you earn a +3 bonus on top.
faq.a_change_picks: Yes, as many times as you like before the match locks. Each match locks 10 minutes before its kickoff — change your mind right up until then.
faq.a_when_join: You can join any time before the entry deadline (3 days after kickoff). Late joiners can still pick unlocked matches but auto-forfeit anything already locked.
faq.a_confidence: When you make a prediction, you can optionally declare how sure you are — from Hopeful (a punt) to Dead Cert (banking on it). Your group sees your confidence after the match. It doesn't affect scoring — it's purely for bragging rights and banter.
faq.q_points_per_match: How many points can I earn per match?
faq.a_points_per_match: Group stage: up to 5 pts (2 for correct outcome + 3 exact score bonus). Knockout: up to 6 pts (2 outcome + 3 score + 1 for correct advancing team).
faq.q_classifications: What are the different leaderboard classifications?
faq.a_classifications: **Overall** — cumulative points across all matches. **Format** — survivor elimination that mirrors the World Cup structure. **Full Bracket** & **KO Bracket** — pre-tournament bracket predictions scored after groups / knockouts complete.
faq.q_draw_knockout: What does “Draw” mean in a knockout match?
faq.a_draw_knockout: It means you're predicting the score will be level after 90 minutes. The match may go to extra time and penalties, but your score prediction is based on the 90-minute whistle only. When you pick Draw, you'll be asked to separately pick which team advances.
faq.q_extra_ko_point: How does the extra knockout point work?
faq.a_extra_ko_point: In knockout matches there's a bonus point for correctly predicting which team advances. If you pick a team to win, they're automatically your advancing pick — no extra step needed. If you pick Draw, you separately choose who goes through. Either way, 1 bonus point if you get it right.
faq.q_postponed: What happens if a match is postponed?
faq.a_postponed: Your picks stay valid. The match moves to its new time and your predictions carry over. If a match is cancelled, affected picks are voided (no points gained or lost).
faq.a_auto_save: Yes. Winner picks save instantly on tap. Score predictions save after 1.75 seconds of inactivity or when you tap away.
faq.a_desktop: Yes — the app works on any device with a browser. It's designed mobile-first but fully functional on desktop.

landing.coming_soon_heading: The World Cup is being set up.
landing.coming_soon_desc: Drop back closer to June. We're seeding the fixtures, polishing the scoring, and getting the chairs out.
landing.wc_desc: Predict every match. 5 ways to compete. Bragging rights guaranteed.
landing.step1_desc: Your mate sends a link. You're in. No downloads, no sign-up forms — just Google log in and go.
landing.step2_desc: Each round has a mix of fixtures across sports. Pick your winners before the deadline locks.
landing.step3_desc: Points land as results come in. See where you stand, who called it, and who got it very wrong.
landing.for_friends_desc: Just the cold, crisp satisfaction of being right.

wc.format_explainer: 48 players. Groups of 4. Finish bottom of your group and you're out. Survivors face knockout rounds where the field halves until only one remains. Pick match winners, guess exact scores for bonus points.
wc.join_desc: Free to play. Bragging rights on the line.

prediction.conflict_body: Your score ({homeScore}–{awayScore}) means {serverWinner} wins, but you picked {enteredPick}.
rules_preview.format_p1: Players are drawn into groups of four. After each prediction window, the bottom player in each group is eliminated. New groups are drawn from the survivors. Last player standing wins.
rules_preview.format_p2: Points reset each window. Only your performance in the current round matters for survival.
rules_preview.format_p3: Groups are drawn 24 hours before the first match of each stage.
rules_preview.overall_tagline: Even if you're out, you're in.
rules_preview.overall_p1: Every point counts across the whole tournament. No elimination, no resets. Eliminated from the Format? Your Overall score keeps ticking. Consistency wins.
rules_preview.bracket_p1: Predict the outcome of every group and every knockout tie before the tournament starts. As results come in, incorrect predictions are knocked out. Players with the most surviving picks lead the table.
rules_preview.ko_bracket_p1: Once the group stage is finalised, predict every knockout tie from the Round of 32 to the Final. Same rules as the Full Bracket, but with the advantage of knowing who actually qualified.
group.consolidated: Your group was consolidated before kickoff for competitive balance.
group.remainder_hint: More players may still join. If fewer than 3 players are in this group at kickoff, you'll be moved to a nearby group.
group.sizing_rule: Groups are 4 players each. If the total doesn't divide evenly, the last 1 or 2 players are added to existing groups. A remainder of 3 stays as its own group.
standings.kickoff_in: Standings open when results land. Kickoff in {days} {dayLabel}.
